data.title: Stablecoins are key to DeFi, moving more value than Bitcoin & Ethereum combined by 2025. U.S. lawmakers aim to regulate stablecoins under the "GENIUS Act," focusing on backing, audits, and licensing to avoid collapses like Terra's. Regulation will impact DeFi liquidity and asset usability. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Stablecoins have quietly become a key component of the crypto market, surpassing Bitcoin & Ethereum in on-chain value by 2025. Regulators are now focusing on stablecoin rules, with U.S lawmakers working on the "GENIUS Act" for a unified framework. These rules will impact DeFi liquidity, protocol revenues, and asset usability. Stablecoins are crucial for DeFi operations, with trillions of dollars processed annually by late 2025. Regulation primarily targets stablecoin issuers, emphasizing full reserve backing, audits, transparency, and oversight. This affects which stablecoins DeFi protocols can rely on.ETH remains primary settlement despite Layer 2 network growth. Stablecoin rules will have a significant impact on DeFi operations.

data.title: Job data in December solidifies Fed's cautious stance on future rate cuts. Unemployment must show more significant increase for cuts to happen.
data.description: In order for the central bank to consider reducing interest rates once more, officials will probably require further significant indications of an increase in unemployment rates.

data.title: Ping An Biomedical Co., Ltd (NASDAQ: PASW) to invest in Future Biotechnology Group for AI-driven R&D synergy in biopharma.
data.description: Ping An Biomedical, listed on NASDAQ as PASW, has announced a significant investment in Future Biotechnology Group. This investment aims to boost AI-driven research and development in the biopharmaceutical sector. The collaboration between the two companies seeks to enhance industry synergy and drive advancements in the field. This move underscores the commitment to innovation and growth, with the potential to significantly impact the biotech sector.

data.title: United Community Banks (NYSE: UCB) to report Q4 earnings Jan. 14, 2025. Analysts predict 71 cents per share, revenue $273.6 million, up from $251.93 million. Repurchase up to $100 million in common stock authorized.
data.description: United Community Banks, Inc. (NYSE: UCB) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on Wednesday, Jan. 14, 2025, before the market opens. Analysts are anticipating earnings of 71 cents per share, a jump from 63 cents per share reported in the same quarter last year. The consensus among analysts for the company's quarterly revenue stands at $273.6 million, reflecting growth from $251.93 million recorded a year ago. United Community Banks recently approved a buyback plan worth $100 million for its common stock. Following the news, the company's shares saw a 2.3% increase in value.

data.title: US economy adds 50,000 jobs in December; unemployment rate falls to 4.4%. Wage growth accelerates.
data.description: An updated report from the latest U.S. labor market data revealed that the U.S. economy concluded 2025 with a more stable labor market than anticipated, alleviating concerns over escalating job losses in the late summer and early fourth quarter. In December, nonfarm payrolls saw an increase of 50,000 jobs, a slight deviation from November's revised gain of 56,000, and slightly below the projected 60,000 growth. The Bureau of Labor Statistics disclosed that October's payrolls were adjusted down by 68,000, showcasing deeper job losses from 105,000 to 173,000. The unemployment rate decreased from a revised 4.5% in November to 4.4%, falling under the expected 4.5%. Additionally, wage growth showed improvement as average hourly earnings rose by 0.3% in December, outpacing the 0.2% increase in November and aligning with predictions. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Jamf named Leader in 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Endpoint Management Tools, only Apple-focused platform.
data.description: Jamf, a company listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol JAMF, headquartered in Minneapolis, has been acknowledged as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Endpoint Management Tools. This recognition is based on criteria evaluating Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute. Jamf stands out as the sole Apple-centric platform offering a comprehensive suite of management, security, and AI-driven automation solutions. This enables organizations to benefit from native Apple integration, top-tier security measures, and effortless compatibility with their current systems.
